author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Luke xiv. 16, ‘A certain man made a great supper,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke xiv. 16, ‘A certain man made a great supper,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Delivered in the basilica Restituta.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Holy lessons have been set forth before us, to which we should both give ear, and upon which by the Lord’s help I would deliver some observations.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the Apostolic lesson thanks are rendered unto the Lord for the faith of the Gentiles, of course, because it was His work.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the Psalm we have said, “O God of hosts, turn us, and show us Thy Face, and we shall be saved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the Gospel we have been called to a supper; yea, rather others have been called, we not called, but led; not only led, but even forced.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so have we heard, that “a certain Man made a great supper.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is this Man, but “the Mediator between God and men, the Man Christ Jesus”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He sent that those who had been invited might come, for the hour was now come, that they should come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who are they who had been invited, but those who had been called by the Prophets who were sent before?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of old, ever since the Prophets were sent, they invited to Christ’s supper.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They were sent then to the people of Israel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Often were they sent, often did they call men, to come at the hour of supper.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they received those who invited them, refused the supper.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What means “they received those who invited them, refused the supper”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They read the Prophets and killed Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when they killed Him, then though they knew it not, they prepared a Supper for us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the Supper was now prepared, when Christ had been offered up, when the Supper of the Lord, which the faithful know, had been set forth after the resurrection of Christ, and established by His Hands and Mouth, were the Apostles sent to them, to whom the Prophets had been sent before.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Come ye to the supper.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who would not come made excuses.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how did they excuse themselves?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There were three excuses: “One said, I have bought a farm, and I go to see it; have me excused.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Another said, I have bought five pairs of oxen, and I go to prove them; I pray thee have me excused.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A third said, I have married a wife, have me excused; I cannot come.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do we suppose that these are not the excuses, which hinder all men, who decline to come to this supper?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us look into them, discuss, find them out; but only that we may beware.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the purchase of the farm, the spirit of domination is marked out; therefore pride is rebuked.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For men are delighted to have a farm, to hold, to possess it, to have men in it under them, to have dominion.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: An evil vice, the first vice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the first man wished to have dominion, in that he would not that any should have dominion over him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is to have dominion, but to take pleasure in one’s own power?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is a greater power, let us submit ourselves to it, that we may be able to be safe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I have bought a farm, have me excused.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Having discovered pride, he would not come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Another said, I have bought five pairs of oxen.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Would it not have been enough, “I have bought oxen”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Something beyond doubt there is, which by its very obscurity challenges us to seek out, and understand; and in that it is shut, He exhorteth us to knock.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The five pairs of oxen are the senses of this body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There are numbered five senses of this body, as is known to all; and they who, it may be, do not consider it, will doubtless perceive it on being reminded of it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There are then found to be five senses of this body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the eyes is the sight, the hearing in the ears, the smell in the nose, the taste in the mouth, the touch in all the members.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have perception of white and black, and things coloured in whatever way, light and dark, by the sight.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Harsh and musical sounds, we have perception of by the hearing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of sweet and offensive smells, we have perception by the smell.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of things sweet and bitter by the taste.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of things hard and soft, smooth and rough, warm and cold, heavy and light, by the touch.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are five, and they are pairs.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now that they are pairs, is seen most easily in the case of the three first senses.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There are two eyes, two ears, two nostrils; see three pairs.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the mouth, that is in the sense of taste, a certain doubling is found, because nothing affects the taste, unless it is touched by the tongue and the palate.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The pleasure of the flesh which pertains to the touch, has this doubling in a less obvious way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there is both an outer and an inner touch.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so it too is double.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why are they called pairs of oxen?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because by these senses of the body, earthly things are sought for.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For oxen turn up the earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So there are men far off from faith, given up to earthly things, occupied in the things of the flesh; who will not believe anything but what they attain to by the five senses of their body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In those five senses do they lay down for themselves the rules of their whole will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I will not believe,” says one, “anything but what I see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, here is what I know, and am sure of.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such a thing is white, or black, or round, or square, or coloured so and so; this I know, am sensible of, have a hold of; nature itself teaches it me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am not forced to believe what you cannot show me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or it is a voice: I perceive that it is a voice; it sings well, it sings ill, it is sweet, it is harsh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I know, I know this, it has come to me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is a good or a bad smell: I know, I perceive it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is sweet, this is bitter; this is salt, this insipid.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I know not what you would tell me more.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By the touch I know what is hard, what is soft; what is smooth, what is rough; what is warm, and what cold.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What more would you show me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By such an impediment was our Apostle Thomas held back, who as to the Lord Christ, the resurrection that is of Christ, would not believe even his own eyes only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Unless,” says he, “I put my fingers into the places of the nails and wounds, and unless I put my hand into His side, I will not believe.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Lord who could have risen again without any vestige of a wound, kept the scars, that they might be touched by the doubting Apostle, and the wounds of his heart be healed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet as designing to call to His supper others, against the excuse of “the five pairs of oxen,” He said, “Blessed they who do not see, and believe.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We, my Brethren, who have been called to this supper, have not been kept back by “these five pairs.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we have not in this age desired to see the Face of the Lord’s Body, nor have we longed to hear the Voice proceeding out of the mouth of that Body; we have not sought in Him for any passing odour.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A certain “woman anointed Him with most costly ointment,” that “house was filled with the odour;” but we were not there; lo, we did not smell, yet we believe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He gave to the disciples the Supper consecrated by His Own Hands; but we did not sit down at that Feast, and yet we daily eat this same Supper by faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And do not think it strange that in that supper which He gave with His Own Hand, one was present without faith: the faith that appeared, afterwards was more than a compensation for that faithlessness then.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Paul was not there who believed, Judas was there who betrayed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How many now too in this same Supper, though they saw not then that table, nor beheld with their eyes, nor tasted with their mouths, the bread which the Lord took in His Hands, yet because it is the same as is now prepared, how many now also in this same Supper, “eat and drink judgment to themselves”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But whence arose an occasion, so to say, to the Lord, to speak of this supper?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One of them that sat at meat with Him (for He was at a feast, whither He had been invited), had said, “Blessed are they who eat bread in the kingdom of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He sighed as though after distant things, and the Bread Himself was sitting down before him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is the Bread of the kingdom of God, but He who saith, “I am the Living Bread which came down from heaven”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not get thy mouth ready, but thine heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: On this occasion it was that the parable of this supper was set forth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, we believe in Christ, we receive Him with faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In receiving Him we know what to think of.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We receive but little, and we are nourished in heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is not then what is seen, but what is believed, that feeds us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore we too have not sought for that outward sense; nor have we said, “Let them believe who have seen with their eyes, and handled with their hands the Lord Himself after His resurrection, if what is said be true; we do not touch Him, why should we believe?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we were to entertain such thoughts, we should be kept back from the supper by those “five pairs of oxen.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That ye may know, Brethren, that not the gratification of these five senses, which softens and ministers pleasure, but a kind of curiosity was denoted, He did not say, “‘I have bought five pairs of oxen,’ and I go to feed them;” but, “I go to prove them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who wishes to “prove” by “the pairs of oxen,” does not wish to be in doubt, just as St. Thomas by these “pairs” did not wish to be in doubt.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let me see, let me touch, let me put in my fingers.” “‘Behold,’ saith the Lord, ‘put in thy fingers along My Side, and be not unbelieving.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thy sake have I been slain; at the place which thou wishest to touch, have I shed My Blood, that I might redeem thee; and dost thou still doubt of Me, unless thou touch Me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Behold, this too I grant; behold, this too I show thee; touch, and believe; find out the place of My wound, heal the wound of thy doubting.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The third said, I have married a wife.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the pleasure of the flesh, which is a hindrance to many: and I would that it were so only without, and not within!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There are men who say, “There is no happiness for a man, if he have not the pleasures of the flesh.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These are they whom the Apostle censures, saying, “‘Let us eat and drink, for to-morrow we shall die.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who hath risen to this life from the other?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who hath ever told us what goes on there?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We take away with us, what in the time present makes our happiness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He that speaks thus, “has married a wife,” attaches himself to the flesh, places his delight in the pleasures of the flesh, excuses himself from the supper; let him look well to it that he die not by an inward famine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend to John, the holy Apostle and Evangelist; “Love not the world, neither the things that are in the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O ye who come to the Supper of the Lord, “Love not the world, neither the things that are in the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not say, “Have not;” but, “Love not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast had, possessed, loved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The love of earthly things, is the bird-lime of the spirit’s wings.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, thou hast desired, thou hast stuck fast.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Who will give thee wings as of a dove?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When wilt thou fly, whither thou mayest in deed, seeing thou hast perversely wished to rest here, where thou hast to thy hurt stuck fast?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Love not the world,” is the divine trumpet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By the voice of this trumpet unceasingly is it proclaimed to the compass of the earth, and to the whole world, “Love not the world, neither the things that are in the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whosoever loveth the world, the love of the Father is not in him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For all that is in the world, is the lust of the flesh, and the lust of the eyes, and the ambition of life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He begins at the last with which the Gospel ends.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He begins at that, at which the Gospel made an end.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The lust of the flesh, I have married a wife.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The lust of the eyes, I have bought five pairs of oxen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The ambition of life, I have bought a farm.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now these senses are denoted by the mention of the eyes only, the whole by a part, because the pre-eminence in the five senses belongs to the eyes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore though sight belongs peculiarly to the eyes, we are accustomed to use the word “seeing” through all the five senses.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the first place, in relation to the eyes themselves we say; “See how white it is, look and see how white it is:” this has relation to the eyes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear and see how musical it is!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Could we say conversely, “Hear and see how white it is”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This expression, “see,” runs through all the senses; whereas the distinguishing expression of the other senses does not in its turn run through it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Mark and see how musical; smell and see how agreeable it is; taste and see how sweet it is; touch and see how soft it is.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet surely since they are senses, we should rather say thus; “Hear and be sensible how musical it is; smell and be sensible how agreeable it is; taste and be sensible how sweet it is; touch and be sensible how hot it is; handle and be sensible how smooth it is; handle and be sensible how soft it is.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But we say none of these.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thus the Lord Himself after His resurrection when He appeared to His disciples, and when though they saw Him they still wavered in faith supposing that they saw a spirit, said, “Why do ye doubt, and why do thoughts arise in your hearts?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See My Hands and My Feet.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is not enough to say, “See;” He saith, “Touch, and handle, and see.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Look and see, handle and see; with the eyes alone see, and see by all the senses.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because He was looking for the inner sense of faith, He offered Himself to the outward senses of the body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have made no attainment in the Lord by these outward senses, we have heard with our ears, have believed with our heart; and this hearing not from His mouth, but from the mouth of His preachers, from their mouths who were already at the supper, and who by the pouring forth of what they there drunk in invited us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us away then with vain and evil excuses, and come we to the supper by which we may be made fat within.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not the puffing up of pride keep us back, let it not lift us up, nor unlawful curiosity scare us, and turn us away from God; let not the pleasure of the flesh hinder us from the pleasure of the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us come, and be filled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And who came but the beggars, the “maimed,” the “halt,” the “blind”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But there came not thither the rich, and the whole, who walked, as they thought, well, and saw acutely; who had great confidence in themselves, and were therefore in the more desperate case, in proportion as they were more proud.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the beggars come, for He inviteth them, “who, though He was rich, for our sakes became poor, that we beggars through His poverty might be enriched.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the maimed come, “for they that are whole need not a physician, but they that are in evil case.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the halt come who may say to Him, “Set in order my steps in Thy paths.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the blind come who may say, “Enlighten mine eyes, that I may never sleep in death.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such as these came at the hour, when those who had been first invited, had been rejected for their own excuses: they came at the hour, they entered in from the streets and lanes of the city.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the servant “who had been sent,” brought answer, “Lord, it is done as Thou hast commanded, and yet there is room.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Go out,” saith He, “into the highways and hedges, and compel those whom thou shalt find to come in.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whom thou shalt find wait not till they choose to come, compel them to come in.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I have prepared a great supper, a great house, I cannot suffer any place to be vacant in it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Gentiles came from the streets and lanes: let the heretics come from the hedges, here they shall find peace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For those who make hedges, their object is to make divisions.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them be drawn away from the hedges, let them be plucked up from among the thorns.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They have stuck fast in the hedges, they are unwilling to be compelled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us come in, they say, of our own good will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is not the Lord’s order, “Compel them,” saith he, “to come in.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let compulsion be found outside, the will arise within.
